niche.focus: General educational explainers for demonstrating the video automation workflow.
niche.audience: Curious viewers who want clear, practical explanations of everyday topics.
niche.content_style: Calm narration with sourced visuals, simple diagrams, and concise takeaways.
example_topics: How everyday habits shape your focus
Five small design choices that make tools easier to use
Why simple systems are easier to maintain

visual_guidance.google_photo: Use for specific real-world examples where viewers need to see the actual object, place, or action.
visual_guidance.stock_photo: Use for generic scenes, people, workspaces, and lifestyle examples.
visual_guidance.b_roll: Use for simple motion scenes and transitions.
visual_guidance.ai_photo: Use when a generic but specific visual cannot be sourced.
visual_guidance.ai_illustration: Use for abstract concepts, diagrams, and explanatory support visuals.
visual_guidance.visual_policy: Set visual_policy explicitly when a slot needs exact sourcing or a photo-backed info slide. Do not use text_only_slide outside test previews.

listicle.section_style: One practical idea per section with a clear example and takeaway.
listicle.pacing: Hook the idea, show the example, explain why it matters, then give one practical takeaway.
listicle.example: 5 Small Choices That Make Digital Tools Easier to Use
narrative_hooks: Tease the most surprising item in the opening hook, then deliver the list in order.
Frame the list as a practical checklist viewers can apply immediately.
Open with a common mistake, then reveal the better choice section by section.

explainer.section_style: A clear question broken into causes, examples, and practical implications.
explainer.example: Why Simple Systems Are Easier to Maintain
narrative_hooks: Start with a familiar problem, then explain the hidden mechanism behind it.
Open with a simple question and build toward one useful mental model.
Begin with a counterintuitive claim, then unpack it through concrete examples.

narrative.section_style: Story-driven explanation with a problem, discovery, and useful conclusion.
narrative.pacing: Relatable setup -> failed approach -> better system -> practical lesson.
narrative.example: The Simple Workflow That Saved an Afternoon
narrative_hooks: Start with a relatable moment of friction, then follow the fix.
Tell the story as a before-and-after change in how a task gets done.
Open with the cost of a messy process, then show the simpler replacement.

voice.voice_prompt: Clear, warm narrator. Calm American English, natural pace, practical and friendly.
voice_prompt_variations: Clear, warm narrator. Calm American English, natural pace, practical and friendly.
Measured educational narrator with a concise, helpful tone.
Conversational narrator who sounds focused, calm, and easy to follow.

image_sourcing.style_prompt_suffix: clean editorial photo, natural lighting, uncluttered composition, modern educational media style
image_sourcing.illustration_style_prompt_suffix: clean educational illustration, simple shapes, warm balanced colors, no text

title_formats.name: question
title_formats.instruction: Title MUST be a clear question.

title_formats.name: numbered_list
title_formats.instruction: Title MUST start with a number.

title_formats.name: hook
title_formats.instruction: Title MUST use a practical curiosity hook.

description_styles.name: short_clear
description_styles.instruction: Write 2-3 concise sentences about what the video covers and end with a simple subscribe CTA.

description_styles.name: timestamps
description_styles.instruction: Write one short paragraph, then list section timestamps in 00:00 format.

script_style.instructions: Use simple language and concrete examples. Keep the video useful without claiming expertise in regulated fields. Prefer real-world visuals first, then diagrams or info slides when they clarify the idea. Every info_slide must include props.title, props.text, and a prompt for sourced media. Place one subscribe_cta slot after the viewer has received real value.

business_strategy.channel_goal: Demonstrate a configurable content strategy without exposing a real channel plan.
video_jobs: Show that generated videos can stay aligned with a channel strategy.
Keep calls to action simple and non-commercial.

content_families.name: practical_checklist
content_families.planning_focus: A list of practical ideas viewers can apply immediately.
example_topics: 5 Small Choices That Make Digital Tools Easier to Use
content_families.cta_angle: Invite viewers to subscribe for more practical explainers.

content_families.name: concept_explainer
content_families.planning_focus: A clear explanation of one useful concept with everyday examples.
example_topics: Why Simple Systems Are Easier to Maintain
How Feedback Loops Shape Daily Habits
content_families.cta_angle: Invite viewers to subscribe for more clear explanations.

content_families.name: workflow_story
content_families.planning_focus: A short story about a messy process becoming simpler.
example_topics: The Simple Workflow That Saved an Afternoon
How One Checklist Prevented a Week of Rework
content_families.cta_angle: Invite viewers to subscribe for more practical systems.

cta_rules: Primary CTA is a simple subscribe CTA.
Do not invent downloads, products, offers, email lists, or private communities.
business_strategy.launch_strategy: Use this demo strategy only for public examples and tests.

thumbnail_strategies.name: demo_thumbnail
thumbnail_strategies.instruction: Create a clean educational thumbnail with one strong visual subject, high contrast, and the exact thumbnail_text as the only readable text. Keep the layout simple and avoid implying regulated advice, guaranteed results, or platform affiliation.
